data.title: Flora Growth Corp (FLGC) surged 6% in after-hours trading to $10.55. The company aims to raise up to $75 million by selling 30 million shares at $2.50 each via Regulation A filing.
data.description: Flora Growth Corp.'s (NASDAQ: FLGC) stock saw a 5.98% increase in after-hours trading, reaching $10.55. The Florida-based company's stock closed at $9.96 on Thursday, marking a 3.59% rise. Flora Growth filed a Form 1-A with the SEC for an offering of Regulation A securities, planning to sell 30 million common shares at $2.50 each. This move aims to raise up to $75 million in total proceeds. Aegis Capital is... The complete article can be found on the Benzinga website.
Senior economic minister: Indonesia set to announce Sumatra recovery policy measures soon

data.title: Energy companies worldwide awarded at S&P Global Energy's 27th Annual Platts Global Energy Awards. CNBC's Brian Sullivan hosted the event which saw winners from 10 countries.
data.description: Hosted by Brian Sullivan from CNBC, an event was held in New York on December 11, 2025, to announce the winners from 10 different countries. S&P Global Energy (formerly known as S&P Global Commodity Insights), a top global data provider, organized the event. The winners hailed from Australia, Brazil, China, Denmark, India, Singapore, Spain, the UAE, the UK, and the US.

data.title: WSPN obtains ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification, enhancing information security standards. Dec 11, 2025 in Tortola, BVI.
data.description: Today, in Tortola, British Virgin Islands, the Worldwide Stablecoin Payment Network (WSPN) revealed that they have achieved the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification for their Information Security Management System. This accreditation underscores their commitment to ensuring the security of their infrastructure. WSPN, a prominent player in the stablecoin sector, is known for its cutting-edge technology. This accomplishment highlights the company's dedication to maintaining high security standards, reaffirming their position as a trusted provider in the industry.

data.title: Binance partners with Trump's crypto platform, offering zero-fee trading pairs for USD1 stablecoin.
data.description: Binance recently introduced a variety of trading pairs at zero fees for the stablecoin associated with the Trump family. This stablecoin is now being utilized to support their own stablecoin collateral.

data.title: Scott Bessent criticizes Senator Elizabeth Warren for harming American economy, accusing her of sabotaging foreign investment under Trump. Warren requested information from UBS about potential HQ relocation to the US. Bessent condemns Warren for undermining Trump's 'golden age'.
data.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticized Senator Elizabeth Warren, accusing her of harming the American economy and undermining the prosperous era under President Donald Trump. Warren was accused of hindering Foreign Direct Investment (FDI). Bessent condemned Warren for requesting information from UBS Group AG, questioning interactions with Trump administration officials regarding potential relocation to the U.S. and offered incentives. Bessent argued Warren's actions were partisan. The criticism came after reports of Warren's letter to UBS Chairman Colm Kelleher, covered by Reuters. Bessent emphasized Trump's tariff structure permanence despite any Supreme Court decision. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Treasure Global closed USD 2.5 million direct offering of 250,000 shares of common stock to institutional investors.
data.description: In Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, on December 11, 2025, Treasure Global Inc. (NASDAQ: TGL), a technology company based in Southeast Asia, finalized a registered direct offering with institutional investors. The offering involved the purchase and sale of 250,000 shares of its common stock with a par value of $0.00001 per share. The company generated total gross proceeds of $2,500,000 from this transaction, excluding commissions and offering expenses.
Singapore sees $6.2B private real estate fund from Hongkong Land

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci praises JPMorgan for issuing $50 million in U.S. commercial paper on Solana blockchain, supporting his investment thesis.
data.description: SkyBridge Capital's founder, Anthony Scaramucci, expressed enthusiasm on Thursday regarding JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s decision to issue debt securities through Solana. This move aligns with Scaramucci's investment perspective. JPMorgan's issuance of $50 million in U.S. commercial paper for Galaxy Digital Inc. directly on Solana marks a significant event as a major global bank embraces a public blockchain for security issuance. Interestingly, this initiative follows JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon's recent statement about exploring blockchain technology for client service enhancements. Scaramucci, a prominent cryptocurrency supporter, welcomed this development as a positive indicator for his investment strategy focused on Solana.

data.title: Binance aligns with Trump's crypto platform, incorporating USD1 stablecoin pairs for zero-fee trading with backing support.
data.description: Binance recently introduced several trading pairs with zero fees for the Trump family's stablecoin. This move is part of Binance's strategy to enhance its stablecoin collateral by utilizing the Trump family's stablecoin. The initiative aims to improve trading efficiency and reduce costs for users.
Markets react to Fed easing; gold prices soar

data.title: T1 Energy prices public offerings of $140M in convertible notes due 2030 & 28.28M shares of common stock at $4.95/share. Upsized from $120M previously announced.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, T1 Energy Inc. (NYSE: TE) based in Austin, Texas and New York, disclosed the pricing details of their recent public offerings. The offerings included $140.0 million aggregate principal amount of 5.25% convertible senior notes due 2030 (referred to as the “Convertible Notes Offering”) and 28,282,830 shares of common stock priced at $4.95 per share (known as the “Common Stock Offering”). The Convertible Notes Offering expanded from the initial $120.0 million aggregate principal amount.

data.title: Popping candy company in China shifts to "Intelligent Manufacturing" as global OEM/ODM strategy. #Globalization #Baida
data.description: In Chaozhou, China, on December 11, 2025, it was reported that with the advancement of globalization, the label of "Made in China" is shifting towards "Intelligent Manufacturing in China." Baida Popping Candy, a prominent player in the global popping candy industry, is at the forefront of this transformation. The company is actively incorporating smart technologies into its production processes to enhance efficiency.
